overview: The witest28-5 system is a minimal single-component architecture consisting of a Go-based HTTP server that serves a static "Hello World" web page. The server handles all routing internally, returning a valid HTML page at the root path, a /health endpoint for liveness checks, and a proper 404 response for all undefined routes. There are no inter-service dependencies or external integrations.
requirements: The root URL (/) must return HTTP 200 with an HTML page prominently displaying the text 'Hello World'.
The HTML page must include a valid document structure: <html>, <head>, <body>, and <title> elements.
The browser tab title must read 'Hello World' (or clearly identify the application).
The page must render correctly without JavaScript enabled.
The page must be responsive and readable on desktop and mobile screen sizes.
The greeting text must meet WCAG AA contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background.
The page must produce zero browser console errors on load.
The page must fully load within 2 seconds on a broadband connection (≥ 10 Mbps).
Requests to undefined routes must return HTTP 404.
The application must not expose server-side error messages or stack traces to users.
The page must not include any third-party scripts.
The HTML must be valid and pass W3C Markup Validation with no errors.
A /health endpoint must return HTTP 200 to support liveness/readiness probes.
The application must target 99% monthly uptime.

components.name: hello-world-server
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Server API
  description: Serves the Hello World web page and supporting endpoints.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the Hello World HTML page with HTTP 200.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page containing the Hello World greeting.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns HTTP 200 when the service is healthy.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all 404
      description: Returns HTTP 404 for all undefined routes.
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Route not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
## Component: hello-world-server ### Purpose A lightweight Go HTTP server that serves a static Hello World HTML page. No database, no external dependencies, no JavaScript required. ### Language & Framework - **Language:** Go (1.22+) - **HTTP library:** Standard library `net/http` only — no third-party frameworks. - **Port:** 9090 ### Project Structure ``` /hello-world-server main.go Dockerfile go.mod ``` ### Routing | Method | Path | Behavior | |--------|-----------|----------| | GET | / | Return 200 with Hello World HTML page | | GET | /health | Return 200 with JSON `{"status": "ok"}` | | GET | * | Return 404 with a minimal HTML error page (no stack traces, no server info) | Register `/health` first, then `/` explicitly. Use a catch-all handler for everything else. The catch-all must check that the path is NOT `/` before returning 404 (to avoid double-handling), or use an explicit mux that routes `/` exactly. ### HTML Page Requirements (`/`) The HTML response must: - Use `Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8` - Be fully valid W3C HTML5 (no errors) - Include: ``, ``, ``, `Hello World`, ``, ``, ``, `
`, `

Hello World

` - Have NO inline or external JavaScript whatsoever - Have NO external CSS or script links (fonts, CDNs, analytics, etc.) - Use only inline ` ``` ### 404 Page Requirements - Return HTTP status 404 - Return a minimal valid HTML5 page with title "Not Found" - No stack traces, no server info, no error detail beyond "Page not found" - Same inline-only style approach, no external resources ### /health Endpoint - Return HTTP 200 - `Content-Type: application/json` - Body: `{"status":"ok"}` ### Security Headers Set the following response headers on ALL responses: ``` X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff X-Frame-Options: DENY Referrer-Policy: no-referrer Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'none'; style-src 'unsafe-inline' ``` Remove or do not set the `Server` header. ### main.go Implementation Notes - Create a custom `http.ServeMux`. - Register `GET /health` → health handler. - Register `GET /` exactly → home handler (Go 1.22 pattern `"GET /"` matches only root if using `ServeMux` with exact match; use `r.URL.Path != "/"` guard inside the handler to redirect/404 non-root paths if using older mux style). - Register a catch-all `"GET /"` or fallback handler that returns 404 for any other path. - Wrap all handlers with a middleware that sets security headers. - Use `log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":9090", mux))`. - Graceful shutdown is optional but welcome (listen for SIGTERM/SIGINT, shutdown with 10s timeout). ### go.mod ``` module github.com/witest28-5/hello-world-server go 1.22 ``` No external dependencies. ### Dockerfile ```dockerfile FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder WORKDIR /app COPY go.mod ./ RUN go mod download COPY . . RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -trimpath -ldflags='-s -w' -o server . FROM scratch COPY --from=builder /app/server /server EXPOSE 9090 ENTRYPOINT ["/server"] ``` Using `scratch` base image for minimal attack surface. ### Edge Cases - `GET /` with trailing query string (e.g., `/?foo=bar`) → still return 200 Hello World page. - `GET /ANYTHING_ELSE` → 404. - All HTTP methods other than GET on `/` → return 405 Method Not Allowed (Go 1.22 ServeMux handles this automatically when using method-prefixed patterns like `"GET /"`). - No panics under any input — use `recover` middleware if needed. - Do not log request bodies or query parameters to avoid accidental data leakage in logs; log only method, path, and status code.
